ddls:
CREATE TABLE "customers" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "perc_premium_paid_by_cash_credit" REAL /* Percentage of premium paid by cash or credit */,
  "age_in_days" REAL /* Age of the customer in days since policy inception */,
  "income" REAL /* Income of the customer */,
  "no_of_premiums_paid" INTEGER /* Number of premiums paid by the customer */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the customer */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the customer */,
  "occupation" TEXT /* Occupation of the customer */,
  "education_level" TEXT /* Education level of the customer */,
  "marital_status" TEXT /* Marital status of the customer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "payment_history" (
  "payment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each payment record */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the customer associated with the payment record */,
  "count_3_6_months_late" INTEGER /* Count of payments made 3-6 months late */,
  "count_6_12_months_late" INTEGER /* Count of payments made 6-12 months late */,
  "count_more_than_12_months_late" INTEGER /* Count of payments made more than 12 months late */,
  "payment_amount" REAL /* Amount of each payment */,
  "payment_date" TEXT /* Date of each payment */,
  "payment_method" TEXT /* Method of payment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("payment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_payment_history_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "underwriting_scores" (
  "score_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each underwriting score record */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the customer associated with the score */,
  "application_underwriting_score" REAL /* Underwriting score assigned to the customer application */,
  "score_date" TEXT /* Date when the score was assigned */,
  "score_comments" TEXT /* Comments or notes regarding the score */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("score_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_underwriting_scores_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sourcing_channels" (
  "channel_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sourcing channel record */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the customer associated with the sourcing channels */,
  "channel_A" INTEGER /* Indicates if the customer was acquired via channel A */,
  "channel_B" INTEGER /* Indicates if the customer was acquired via channel B */,
  "channel_C" INTEGER /* Indicates if the customer was acquired via channel C */,
  "channel_D" INTEGER /* Indicates if the customer was acquired via channel D */,
  "channel_E" INTEGER /* Indicates if the customer was acquired via channel E */,
  "channel_details" TEXT /* Additional details about the sourcing channel */,
  "acquisition_date" TEXT /* Date when the customer was acquired through the channel */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("channel_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sourcing_channels_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "residence_area_types" (
  "residence_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each residence type record */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the customer associated with the residence type */,
  "type_rural" INTEGER /* Indicates if the residence area is rural */,
  "type_urban" INTEGER /* Indicates if the residence area is urban */,
  "residence_address" TEXT /* Full address of the residence */,
  "residence_zipcode" TEXT /* Zipcode of the residence */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("residence_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_residence_area_types_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "risk_profiles" (
  "risk_profile_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each risk profile record */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the customer associated with the risk profile */,
  "risk_score" REAL /* Calculated risk score based on customer data */,
  "profile_date" TEXT /* Date when the risk profile was created */,
  "risk_category" TEXT /* Category of risk (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("risk_profile_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_risk_profiles_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access record */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer whose data is accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date of access */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "user_role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, analyst) */,
  "access_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of access in minutes */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)
